Scenario.title: Maroochy Water Breach: Energy Grid Disruption via Stolen Devices
Scenario.overview: Energy Simple A cybercriminal group exploited stolen maintenance devices and legacy systems to disrupt energy grid operations, mirroring the 2000 Maroochy Water Breach incident but targeting modern energy infrastructure.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: ["Obsolete products", "Custom APIs", "Loss or Theft of equipment"]

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: An attacker physically stole a maintenance device containing embedded credentials, enabling direct access to energy grid management systems through removable media. This mirrors historical tactics where compromised hardware was used to bypass digital security controls.

stage.name: Execution
stage.description: Exploiting unpatched industrial control systems (ICS) through known vulnerabilities in outdated hardware/software, the attacker executed malicious payloads to take control of critical grid components, leveraging weaknesses in maintenance processes.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: Malicious commands were injected into insecure API endpoints managing power distribution, causing deliberate service disruptions such as localized blackouts or overloads, directly impacting energy delivery operations.

Scenario.summary: The Maroochy-inspired attack leveraged physical theft of maintenance devices and exploitation of legacy systems to disrupt energy infrastructure, demonstrating how outdated practices and hardware vulnerabilities remain critical risks in modern critical infrastructure environments.
